mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive monthly INDPRO changes are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, labor market slack triggers expectations of delayed Fed tightening and weaker consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 6.5% during any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a negative real rate environment that forces capital into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not significant (p > 0.05) in a linear model.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal demand-driven margin compression rather than healthy growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of triggered months when RSAFS increases and CPIAUCSL > 220 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in housing starts (HOUST) reflect strengthening construction activity and durable goods demand, which translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in HOUST are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor markets and rising wage pressure, which lifts corporate operating costs and compresses profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of falling UNRATE (>0.2pp in 3mo) coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating headline inflation (CPIAUCSL) pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 3-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL does not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered episodes over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth (INDPRO) coincides with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS), the joint signal confirms broad-based economic expansion, amplifying risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to produce a positive Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms either single-proxy signal.
